mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes or OpenShift, simplifying the deployment and management of Kafka clusters. It provides a robust solution for managing Kafka resources with Kubernetes-native APIs. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Strimzi repository for Helm.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka-namespace

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka-namespace --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/0.44.0/examples/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml

```

> Deploys a Kafka cluster with persistent storage.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Kafka cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace

kubectl describe kafka my-cluster -n kafka-namespace

```

> Lists Kafka clusters in the specified namespace.
> Describes the Kafka cluster to check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and any data stored in it.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka-namespace

kubectl delete namespace kafka-namespace

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Kafka data. This step upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka-namespace --version 0.44.0

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace

```

> Backs up the current Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Kafka cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not have enough resources allocated or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and configurations in the Kafka CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Kafka broker'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Verify network policies and service configurations to ensure connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Zookeeper is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Zookeeper instance may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Zookeeper pod for errors using 'kubectl logs <zookeeper-pod-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage for Kafka'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The persistent volume claims may not be bound or available.

**Fix:**
Check the status of persistent volume claims using 'kubectl get pvc -n kafka-namespace'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection, and enhanced configuration options.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kafka versions below 2.8.0.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43.0, ensure that your Kafka configurations are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
